vitals.name: Gran Fondo Pekan / Pekan Classic
vitals.location: Royal Town of Pekan, Pahang, Malaysia

adventure.explanation: The 162km granfondo follows the Pahang river borders from Pekan for 41km before turning to a hilly section peaking at 89m, but remains mainly flat and fast with 1072m elevation.[1]

altitude.explanation: The course's highest point reaches only 89m above sea level in the undulating section north of the Pahang river.[1]

climate.explanation: Held in Pekan on June 22, 2025, the event benefits from Malaysia's tropical climate with average highs around 32°C and low rainfall in the dry season.[1]

community.explanation: Pekan, home to Pahang’s royal family, features a royal palace, museums, mosques, and a luxury hotel, fostering a vibrant local food scene with diverse eateries.[1]

elevation.explanation: The granfondo accumulates 1072m of elevation over 162km, while the 93km mediofondo has just 470m by skipping the hilly 41-110km stretch.[1]

expenses.explanation: Entry as a 2025 UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier in Malaysia keeps costs low at approximately RM200-300 for the 162km distance.[1]

experience.explanation: Riders start and finish in the historic Royal Town of Pekan, crossing the Pahang river at 110km for a scenic 50km return on the north bank.[1]

field_depth.explanation: As a new 2025 UCI Gran Fondo World Series event, Pekan Classics draws a competitive yet developing field of men 19-59 and women 19-49 qualifiers.[1]

length.explanation: The granfondo covers 162km from Pekan, doubling the user's ~101 miles estimate, with a 93km mediofondo shortcut after 41km.[1]

logistics.explanation: The June 20-22, 2025 event in Pekan, 45km south of Kuantan, requires travel 130km north of Mersing for start/finish in the royal town.[1]

race_quality.explanation: The flat-and-fast course along Pahang river borders includes a 92km out-and-back before a 50km north-side return to Pekan on June 22, 2025.[1]

technicality.explanation: Mainly flat roads with slight up-and-down to 89m max and 1072m total elevation offer low technical demands on the Pahang river route.[1]

value.explanation: For RM250 entry, the 162km UCI qualifier in royal Pekan delivers 1072m elevation, river scenery, and 2025 Worlds qualification potential.[1]

cultural_impact.explanation: Promotes cycling in Pahang, blending royalty-themed town with global riders.

prestige.explanation: Debuting in 2025 as Pekan Classics in the UCI Gran Fondo World Series, it qualifies top age-group finishers for the Great Ocean Road Worlds.[1]

course_description.character: Flat river-hugging out-and-back with a central loop deviation, featuring moderate rolling hills and short steep pitches amid tropical scenery.

suffering_zones.label: River Border Push (First 40km)
suffering_zones.description: Sustained flat effort along Pahang River banks tests pacing in humid conditions before the loop.

suffering_zones.label: Loop Deviation
suffering_zones.description: Short interior loop introduces the event's 2500ft elevation via rolling short steep hills.

suffering_zones.label: Return River Grind (Last 68km)
suffering_zones.description: Fatigue sets in on the flat return, where heat and wind amplify the distance.

course_description.signature_challenge: Pahang River double-sided routing: 108km flat borders punctuated by a hilly loop for 2500ft gain.

logistics.overview: UCI Gran Fondo weekend event with 80km/120km distances; international field; held in Royal Town of Pekan.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and homestays in Pekan town; book early for June event weekend.
logistics.getting_there: Fly to Kuantan Airport (KUA) 45km away, then taxi/bus to Pekan; Kuala Lumpur 250km north.
logistics.parking: Ample onsite parking at event village in Royal Town of Pekan.
logistics.spectator_info: Prime viewing along Pahang River borders and loop section; family-friendly event atmosphere.

climate.overview: Tropical monsoon climate with warm, humid conditions ideal for road cycling.
climate.wind_conditions: Light river breezes; potential crosswinds on exposed borders.

terrain.terrain_overview: Moderate difficulty: predominantly flat river paths with rolling short steep hills and sustained moderate climbs in the loop.

history.overview: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier attracting international pelotons to Malaysia's Pahang region.
history.growth_trajectory: Rapid rise as Southeast Asia's premier gran fondo, expanding field depth annually.

final_verdict.one_liner: Scenic, affordable UCI gran fondo blending flat speed with moderate Pahang hills.
final_verdict.best_for: Endurance roadies seeking international flavor, value, and tropical vibes.
final_verdict.not_ideal_for: High-altitude chasers or gravel purists wanting technical suffering.
final_verdict.pro_tip: Embrace the humidity - lightweight kit and river-crossing pacing win the day.

citations.title: Gran Fondo Pekan / Pekan Classic - UCI
citations.snippet: Gran Fondo Pekan / Pekan Classic, Royal Town of Pekan, Pahang, Malaysia, 20 Jun - 22 Jun 2025, Class UGF - UCI Gran Fondo World Series.

citations.title: Gran Fondo Pekan / Pekan Classic
citations.snippet: Pekan / Pekan Classic 2026 (2026-06-28) in Pekan, Malaysia! Explore the course, registration details.

citations.title: Pekan Classics with very international field at the start
citations.snippet: The full course follows both sides of the flat borders of the Pahang river during the first 40km and the last 68km and makes a short loop.

citations.title: UCI Pekan Classics - Gran Fondo Guide
citations.snippet: Distances: 80, 120 km. Difficulty: Moderate terrain with rolling short steep hills or moderate, sustained climbs.
